title: mimosa paradoxa
intro: Mimosa paradoxa, also known as the sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. Native to South America, it is known for its compound leaves that fold up when touched, providing protection from herbivores. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other medicinal compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Mimosa paradoxa, such as infusions and extracts, are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support immune function, making it a popular plant in traditional medicine.
